what_it_does: CSS stylesheet that frames a session-drill modal overlay: scrim, panel, header, search filter, session roster rows, and detail/recall lenses. It relies on design tokens and avoids bespoke values.
observations: Implements an overlay modal with .sessions-overlay, .sessions-scrim, and .sessions-panel.
Uses open/closed state classes (.sessions-overlay--open) to toggle opacity, visibility, and pointer-events.
Applies safe-area insets via --safe-t and --safe-b in padding.
Scrim uses color-mix(in srgb, var(--ink-primary) 48%, transparent).
Search input uses a pill radius and focus-visible outline.
Roster rows are styled as clickable cards with hover state.
Detail view includes .sessions-lens sections with headings and bulleted lists.
Comments explicitly state this is a functional host frame and that DNA owns the session-card chrome.
Most sizing, spacing, color, and typography use CSS custom-property tokens.
standouts: Safe-area padding is explicitly wired into the overlay and body.
The scrim uses color-mix with a token at 48% opacity.
Touch targets enforce min-height: 44px on buttons and rows.
The file claims a 'tokens only' constraint.
conventions: Follows a BEM-like naming convention: block .sessions-*, modifiers with --.
Uses semantic token names for color (--ink-*, --ground-*, --hairline), spacing (--s-*), type (--t-*), and elevation (--elev-*).
Mirrors existing overlay/search patterns from comments.
concerns: The close button hardcodes font-size: 15px, contradicting the 'tokens only' claim.
Transitions are hardcoded to 200ms ease with no reduced-motion guard.
.sessions-card is declared with only display: block, leaving its actual chrome undefined here.
No explicit dark-mode or high-contrast adjustments are visible.
notes: A token-only structural CSS frame for a session-recall modal overlay, deliberately leaving the polished session-card chrome to another layer.
questions: What component or script toggles the .sessions-overlay--open class?
Where are --safe-t, --safe-b, and the other custom properties defined?
What does 'DNA' refer to in the comments?
Where is the .sessions-card visual styling completed?
What data populates the 'recall lenses' sections?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the comments describe a token-only functional host frame for the FACE-1 session-drill surface, and the rules only style the shell, search, roster rows, and detail lenses—apart from the 15px close-button font-size.
apparent_themes: Separation of functional frame from decorative chrome
intention_signals: Provide a lightweight, maintainable, token-only stylesheet for a session selection/recall modal so the structural overlay can be composed with a separate design system's card chrome.
novelty: The explicit safe-area padding on overlay and body, the color-mix scrim, and the self-imposed 'tokens only' constraint with one minor exception.
contribution: A complete, token-based CSS shell for an accessible session overlay—scrim, panel, header, search, roster rows, and detail lenses—that a fused system can keep as the structural frame while augmenting or replacing the card chrome.
summary_for_embedding: CSS stylesheet defining the layout, states, and token-based visual frame for a session-drill modal overlay including scrim, panel, search filter, session roster rows, and detail lenses.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to be the functional, design-token-only host frame for a session-recall overlay, delegating polished session-card styling to a separate design layer.
